steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to connect Sherman’s quotation — about maps presenting ‘a powerful visual representation of the transformation of Manchester’s landscape’ — to the increase in the UK’s urban population during the Industrial Revolution. The answer must accurately use both pieces and show how the maps link the two.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Sherman claims that maps of Industrial Revolution-era Manchester, England, present "a powerful visual representation of the transformation of Manchester's landscape" during a time period in which the UK experienced a substantial (2,000%) increase in population.: The 2,000% figure applies to Manchester specifically, not the UK overall. The UK figure is 30%. This misattributes a statistic and creates a factual error. ✗
  • Choice B — Rural populations relocating to cities like Manchester created a phenomenon that Sherman describes as "a powerful visual representation" of how the UK transformed during the Industrial Revolution.: Sherman’s quote describes maps, not the act of rural populations relocating. This distorts what Sherman actually said. ✗
  • Choice C — Sherman claims that period maps of the UK present "a powerful visual representation" of how the country's rural landscape was transformed by population shifts during the Industrial Revolution.: Sherman’s quote refers specifically to maps of Manchester, not maps of the UK broadly. This overgeneralizes her claim. ✗
  • Choice D — The sizeable growth in the UK's urban population during the Industrial Revolution is reflected in before-and-after maps that present, in Sherman's view, "a powerful visual representation" of how the increase transformed Manchester.: Opens with the UK urban population growth (the stat to connect), links it to the before-and-after maps of Manchester, and incorporates Sherman’s exact quote about what those maps show — accurately and completely accomplishing the task. ✓
That is the correct answer.
